definition: The PBoC is the most consequential central bank for industrial commodity markets but is entirely absent from the knowledge base, constituting a genuine analytical failure. China consumes ~55% of global refined copper, ~60% of iron ore, ~55% of aluminum, and PBoC credit impulse leads copper prices by 3-6 months with R-squared of 0.45-0.55.
evidence: China consumes ~55% of global refined copper, ~60% of iron ore, ~55% of aluminum, ~15% of crude oil
PBoC credit impulse leads copper prices by 3-6 months (R² ~0.45-0.55)
Documented by BIS and IMF — empirical and verifiable, not theoretical
Confirmed as genuine analytical failure vs known-but-priced issue in pair_1 debate
open_questions: How does PBoC credit impulse interact with G3 QT effects on commodity markets?
Should PBoC balance sheet dynamics be incorporated as a primary commodity driver in the KB?
Does the PBoC gap extend to other emerging market central banks with significant commodity consumption?
